summary: Any tweet can independently cross the strict character limit, so exact filtering has a linear worst-case inspection lower bound; one character-length predicate per bounded-width row matches it.

optimality.argument: In a worst-case table with no invalid tweet, changing only the final uninspected row to a 16-character value changes the required result, so every one of the R rows must be inspected. A single scan evaluates the bounded VARCHAR(50) content length once per row and therefore takes O(R) time.

replacement_checks.evidence: An adversary may place the only over-limit content in any row; skipping one row cannot distinguish the valid-only table from the table that must return that row's ID.
The query performs one bounded character-count comparison per tweet during a single filter scan and emits qualifying IDs without a required sort.
Authored fixtures cover the public sample, exact 15/16-character boundaries, empty results, all-invalid tables, spaces, exclamation marks, identifier gaps, and mixed lengths.
